'''Jake Mamo''' (born 6 June 1994) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer, who plays as a {{rlp|ce|wg}}er or {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in the [[RFL League 1]].

He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. Mamo has spent time on dual registration from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Mamo was born in [[Gosford|Gosford, New South Wales]], [[Australia]], and is of [[Maltese people|Maltese]] descent.<ref name="timesofmalta">{{cite web|url=http://www.timesofmalta.com/articles/view/20140303/rugby/Malta-to-face-Greece-and-the-Czech-Republic.509006|title=Malta to face Greece and the Czech Republic - timesofmalta.com|publisher=timesofmalta.com|access-date=2014-04-18}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Ourimbah Wyoming Magpies]], [[St Edwards Bears]] and [[The Entrance Tigers]], before being signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
At the end of 2012, Mamo won the [[Brian Carlson|Brian Carlson Club]]-[[Andrew Johns]] Medal as the Knights' best under-17 player.<ref name="theherald">{{cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/2227095/jakes-break-mamo-to-debut-against-broncos/?cs=306|title=Jake's break: Mamo to debut against Broncos | Newcastle Herald|publisher=theherald.com.au|access-date=2014-04-18}}</ref> In 2013 and 2014, he played for the Newcastle Knights' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/m/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150622220803/http://nycdatabase.org/players/m/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=22 June 2015|title=M|publisher=Nycdatabase.org|access-date=2015-06-26}}</ref> At the end of 2013, he won their NYC Players' Player of the Year award and re-signed with the Knights on a two-year contract.<ref name="theherald"/><ref name="newcastleknights">{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/content/knights/club/members-only/knights_insider_matt_gidley.html|title=KNIGHTS INSIDER: Matt Gidley - Knights|publisher=newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2014-04-18|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140320095621/http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/content/knights/club/members-only/knights_insider_matt_gidley.html|archive-date=20 March 2014|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

===2014===
On 15 and 16 February, Mamo played for the Knights in the inaugural [[2014 NRL Auckland Nines|NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|title=SQUADS FOR AUCKLAND NINES|work=Rugby League Week|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160115214258/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/squads-auckland-nines/|archive-date=15 January 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref> After Newcastle's regular winger [[Akuila Uate]] suffered a knee injury, Mamo was selected to make his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut in Round 7 of the [[2014 NRL season]] against the [[Brisbane Broncos]] at [[Newcastle International Sports Centre|Hunter Stadium]], playing on the wing in Newcastle's 32-6 loss.<ref name="newcastleknights2">{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/04/18/late_mail_knights_v_broncos.html|title=LATE MAIL: Knights v Broncos - Knights|publisher=newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2014-04-18}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Michael Carayannis|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/late-nrl-mail-kade-snowden-out-jake-mamo-on-debut-for-knights-20140417-zqvv8.html|title=Late NRL mail: Kade Snowden out, Jake Mamo on debut for Knights|website=Smh.com.au|date=2014-04-17|access-date=2015-12-21}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/barba-brilliant-in-bronco-blitz/tabid/10874/newsid/77750/default.aspx|title=Barba brilliant in Bronco blitz|website=NRL.com|date=2014-04-18|access-date=2015-12-21}}</ref> On 3 May, he played for the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] under-20s team against the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] under-20s team and scored three tries in the 30-8 win, the first hat-trick ever to be scored in an under-20s [[State of Origin series|State of Origin]] match, as well as winning the [[Darren Lockyer]] medal for Man of the Match.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/2014-nsw-u20s-origin-squad-announced/|title=NSW U20s ORIGIN SQUAD ANNOUNCED|work=Rugby League Week|access-date=2015-04-10|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150402115014/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/2014-nsw-u20s-origin-squad-announced/|archive-date=2 April 2015|df=dmy-all}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Brad Walter|url=http://www.watoday.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/mitchell-moses-stars-as-nsw-win-under-20s-origin-clash-20140503-zr41x.html|title=Mitchell Moses stars as NSW win under 20s Origin clash|publisher=Watoday.com.au|date=2014-05-03|access-date=2015-05-31}}</ref> In Round 14 against the [[Sydney Roosters]], he scored his first NRL career in Newcastle's 29-12 loss at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/roosters-run-over-gutsy-knights/tabid/10874/newsid/79388/default.aspx|title=Roosters run over gutsy Knights|website=NRL.com|date=2014-06-14|access-date=2015-12-21}}</ref> On 2 September, he was named at fullback in the 2014 NYC Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|author=Steven Russo|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/holden-team-year-announced/|title=Holden Cup Team Of The Year Announced|publisher=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2014-09-02|access-date=2015-05-31|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150626101814/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/holden-team-year-announced/|archive-date=26 June 2015|df=dmy-all}}</ref> He finished off his debut year in the NRL having played in 7 matches and scoring 2 tries.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY0NC00LS0tLS0tMjA4MDEtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-10-24|access-date=2015-12-25}}</ref> On 18 October, he played on the wing for the [[Junior Kangaroos]] against the [[Junior Kiwis]] at [[Mount Smart Stadium|Mt Smart Stadium]], scoring a try in the Kangaroos' 14–15 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/junior-kangaroos-side-to-face-nz/tabid/10874/newsid/82468/default.aspx|title=Junior Kangaroos side to face NZ|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=2015-03-13}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/09/22/nyc_duo_in_junior_kangaroo_train_on_squad.html|title=Young Knights aplenty in rep teams - Knights|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-05-31}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Sarah Harvey|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/10634289/Junior-Kiwis-edge-Junior-Kangaroos-in-nailbiter|title=Junior Kiwis edge Junior Kangaroos in nailbiter|website=Stuff.co.nz|date=2014-10-18|access-date=2015-12-18}}</ref>

===2015===
Mamo played for the Knights in the [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/2015-auckland-nines-squad-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/83432/default.aspx|title=2015 Auckland Nines squad lists|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=2015-09-26}}</ref> He finished the tournament as equal top try-scorer with 4 tries alongside [[Solomone Kata]], [[Jack Bird]], [[Justin O'Neill]], [[Bryson Goodwin]] and [[Matt King (rugby league)|Matt King]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/2015-nines-team-tournament-named/|title=2015 NINES TEAM OF THE TOURNAMENT NAMED|publisher=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2015-02-01|access-date=2015-05-31}}{{Dead link|date=August 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref> On 8 May, he re-signed with Newcastle on a two-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2015/05/08/snowden_and_mamo_re_.html|title=Snowden and Mamo re-sign - Knights|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-06-26}}</ref> He spent the first half of the season playing in Newcastle's [[New South Wales Cup]] team before earning a recall back to the first-grade team in Round 15 against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/cronulla-sharks-send-newcastle-knights-spiralling-to-ninth-loss-in-last-10-games-20150621-ghtoel.html|title=Cronulla Sharks send Newcastle Knights spiralling to ninth loss in last 10 games|website=Smh.com.au|access-date=2015-12-21}}</ref> He scored a try in the match in which Newcastle  later lost 30-28 at Hunter Stadium.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/knights-v-sharks-five-key-points/tabid/10874/newsid/87287/default.aspx|title=Knights v Sharks: Five Key Points|website=NRL.com|date=2015-06-21|access-date=2015-12-21}}</ref> On 27 September, he played at fullback in the Knights' [[2015 New South Wales Cup#Chart|2015 New South Wales Cup Grand Final]] win over the [[Wyong Roos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2015/09/22/vb_nsw_cup_grand_fin.html|title=VB NSW Cup Grand Final Team List|publisher=NSWRL|access-date=2015-10-15}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Brett Keeble|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/newcastle-knights/newcastle-knights-extend-clint-newtons-career-with-nsw-cup-win-over-wyong-roos-20150927-gjw2gs.html|title=Newcastle Knights extend Clint Newton's career with NSW Cup win over Wyong Roos|publisher=Smh.com.au|access-date=2015-10-15}}</ref> On 4 October, he played at fullback in the Knights' [[2015 NRL State Championship]] match against [[Queensland Cup]] premiers [[Ipswich Jets]], where in the second half, he left the field concussed after being blindsided by a punch from Jets forward Billy McConnachie following a scuffle with Jets hooker [[Matt Parcell]]. The Knights went on to lose the match 26-12.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/nsw-cup-2015-billy-mcconnachie-sorry-for-jake-mamo-king-hit/news-story/7c1abad40c68b971682376cf6b2573b4|title=Billy McConnachie Sorry for Jake Mamo King Hit|website=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2015-12-21}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/nsw-cup-2015-ipswich-jets-defeat-newcastle-knights-2612-at-anz-stadium/news-story/6ec47d76ca6e7dd372d6e705bb426b29|title=Jets Defeat Knights 26-12 at ANZ Stadium|website=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2015-12-21}}</ref> Mamo finished off the [[2015 NRL season|2015 season]] having played in nine matches and scoring four tries for Newcastle as the club finished last.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY2MC00LS0tLS0tMjA4MDEtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-10-24|access-date=2015-12-25}}</ref>

===2016===
In February, Mamo played for Newcastle in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-announce-nines-squad/|title=Knights Announce Nines Squad - Rugby League Week|website=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2016-01-28|access-date=2016-02-04|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160204042513/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-announce-nines-squad/|archive-date=4 February 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref> He finished the [[2016 NRL season|2016 season]] having played in 13 matches and scoring five tries.  The club would finish last again in 2016 with all of Mamo's 13 games ending in defeat.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY3Ny00LS0tLS0tMjA4MDEtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2016-09-07}}</ref> In December, he signed a two-year contract with [[Super League]] side [[Huddersfield Giants]], after gaining a release from the final year of his Newcastle contract.<ref>{{cite web|author=NewcastleKnights.com.au|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/huddersfield-sign-jake-mamo-knights-release-24777/|title=Huddersfield sign Jake Mamo after Knights release|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2016-08-28|access-date=2016-12-20}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|title=The worst teams in NRL history|website=Sporting News|access-date=7 January 2020|archive-date=11 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191211141048/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2017===
In April 2017, Mamo made his debut for Huddersfield against [[Catalans Dragons]]. After scoring a try, he went on to win Man of the Match, despite the team losing 29-22.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===2018===
Mamo played 16 times for Huddersfield in 2018, scoring 5 tries. His last appearance for the Giants was in the 22-12 defeats at the home if [[Catalan Dragons]] in September 2018.

In November 2018, [[Warrington Wolves]] announced the signing of Mamo on a one-year deal. His initial one-year deal with the [[Warrington Wolves]] was announced at the club's 2019 kit launch in November 2018.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===2019===
Mamo made his first appearance for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Ryan Atkins]] testimonial game against the [[Widnes Vikings]], scoring a [[hat-trick]] in the process

He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>

===2020===
Mamo played ten games for Warrington in the [[2020 Super League season]] as the club qualified for the playoffs but were eliminated in the first week by [[Hull F.C.]]{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===2021===
In round 8 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Mamo scored two tries for Warrington in a 62-18 victory over Salford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57246596|title= Salford Red Devils 18-62 Warrington Wolves|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In round 12, Mamo scored a [[hat-trick]] in Warrington's 40-14 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/57572098|title=Mamo hat trick helps Wire beat Wigan|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 1 July, Mamo signed a three-year deal to join the [[Castleford Tigers]] starting in 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57687823|title=Castleford Tigers sign centre on three-year deal from Warrington Wolves|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

=== 2022 ===
Mamo was given squad number 4 for Castleford, and made his first appearance for the club on 11 February against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-02-11 |title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 16-26 Salford Red Devils |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60320580 |access-date=2022-03-27 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref> He scored his first try for Castleford against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] on 17 March.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2022-03-17 |title=Super League: Wigan Warriors 32-22 Castleford Tigers - Hosts hold off 12-man Cas comeback |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/60788320 |access-date=2022-03-27 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 30 May, it was announced Mamo would miss two of Castleford's matches after he was granted compassionate leave to return to Australia.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jake-mamo-castleford-tigers-compassionate-24102818/|title=Castleford Tigers star Jake Mamo given permission to return to Australia|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk|access-date=19 July 2022|archive-date=10 June 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220610052649/https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/jake-mamo-castleford-tigers-compassionate-24102818|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2023===
On 15 March, [[Castleford Tigers]] announced that Mamo had left the squad with immediate effect, and this was later confirmed to be due to a back injury.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64977310|title=Castleford Tigers confirm Australian back's retirement|website=BBC Sport|date=15 March 2023}}</ref>
On the same day, Mamo announced his retirement from rugby league.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-jake-mamo-australia-29468491/|title=Castleford Tigers'star Jake Mamo, 28, forced to retire after head and back injuries|website=www.mirror.co.uk}}</ref>

===2024===
On 4 August it was reported he had come out of retirement to join [[Midlands Hurricanes]] in the [[RFL League 1]] until the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/wolves/wolvesnews/24496831.jake-mamo-returns-rugby-league-midlands-hurricanes/|title=Jake Mamo returns to rugby league with Midlands Hurricanes |website=Warrington Guardian|date=6 Aug 2024}}</ref>
